voltage-lime.$description: Voltage Lime — Primary CTA fill, active state, accent badges, and decorative highlights — the single chromatic moment in an otherwise monochrome system. Sits at 18.2:1 contrast against black, so it doubles as a high-visibility call-to-action and a hover indicator without needing a second hue

obsidian.$description: Obsidian — Primary text, hairline borders, icon strokes, image borders, and footer background. Carries 2,045 border usages — this system uses black lines to structure space more than boxes or cards

graphite.$description: Graphite — Secondary text and softer borders — slightly lifted from pure black to create a visible hierarchy without introducing color

carbon.$description: Carbon — Icon strokes, link underlines, and low-priority UI marks — the third step of the dark scale, reserved for fine detail that should recede from primary text

paper-white.$description: Paper White — Primary page and card surface — the canvas everything else is drawn onto

concrete.$description: Concrete — Soft section background, alternate surface, and quiet card fill.

whyteregular.$description: WhyteRegular — detected in extracted data but not described by AI

whyte.$description: Display and section headings — used at 72px hero scale and 32px section scale. Tight 1.1 leading and a regular (not bold) weight are anti-convention: most sports brands shout with 800-weight display type, but Whyte Regular at 72px carries authority through letterform precision and negative space alone. The headlines never need a second style. Substitute: Inter Tight or Neue Haas Grotesk Display Pro at 400.

whyte-book.$description: Universal workhorse — body copy, subheadings, product captions, navigation, and buttons. One weight, one family, used at seven sizes. The 38px tier handles product card titles; 16px is the default body; 13px is the fine-print and footer size. Line-height tightens from 1.60 at body to 1.30 at 38px. Substitute: Inter or Söhne at 400.

whyte-inktrap.$description: Secondary detail typeface with inktrap terminals — used for fine UI labels, tag monograms, and small monospace-feeling metadata. The inktrap cuts prevent the letterforms from filling in at small sizes, giving tags and micro-copy a distinct technical voice. Substitute: JetBrains Mono or IBM Plex Mono at 400.

paper-white.$description: Surface level 0: Primary page canvas — all sections, cards, and product grids sit on this surface.

concrete.$description: Surface level 1: Soft section backdrop and divider wash when a page needs a quiet tonal break without introducing a color.

voltage-lime.$description: Surface level 2: Accent surface — appears only on the pill CTA and occasionally as a 12px swatch tag on product images.

obsidian.$description: Surface level 3: Inverted surface for the footer band — black canvas, white type, same border and radius rules.
